name: St. Joseph
title: Foster Father of Jesus
short_bio: St. Joseph is the just man chosen to guard Jesus and Mary. Scripture portrays him as obedient, humble, and decisive in difficult moments.

name: St. Monica
title: Mother of St. Augustine
short_bio: St. Monica prayed for years for her son Augustine's conversion. Her perseverance and tears became a model of patient intercession.

name: St. Augustine
title: Bishop and Doctor of the Church
short_bio: After a restless youth, Augustine converted and became one of the Church's most influential theologians. His writings explore grace, desire, and conversion.

name: St. Thérèse of Lisieux
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: St. Thérèse taught the 'little way' of holiness through ordinary acts done with great love. Her trust in God's mercy remains globally beloved.

name: St. Ignatius of Loyola
title: Founder of the Jesuits
short_bio: A former soldier, Ignatius converted after injury and developed the Spiritual Exercises. He is known for discernment of spirits and mission.

name: St. Francis of Assisi
title: Founder of the Franciscans
short_bio: Francis embraced evangelical poverty and joyful simplicity after a profound conversion. He is remembered for peace-making and love of creation.

name: St. Thomas Aquinas
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Thomas Aquinas integrated faith and reason in a lasting theological synthesis. His humility and devotion accompanied exceptional intellectual work.

name: St. Gianna Molla
title: Wife, Mother, and Physician
short_bio: Gianna Molla was an Italian doctor and mother who integrated professional service with family life. She is honored for heroic love and fidelity to life.

name: St. Dymphna
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: St. Dymphna is venerated as a patron for those experiencing anxiety and mental distress. Her witness is associated with compassion and refuge.

name: St. John Vianney
title: Curé of Ars
short_bio: A parish priest known for long hours in the confessional and pastoral charity, John Vianney became a model for priestly holiness.

name: St. Faustina Kowalska
title: Apostle of Divine Mercy
short_bio: St. Faustina recorded messages emphasizing trust in Jesus' mercy. Her diary shaped modern devotion to Divine Mercy.

name: St. Padre Pio
title: Capuchin Priest
short_bio: Padre Pio was known for prayer, spiritual counsel, and love for the Eucharist. Many sought his intercession in suffering.

name: St. Elizabeth Ann Seton
title: Religious Founder
short_bio: A widow and mother who founded a teaching community, Elizabeth Ann Seton advanced Catholic education in the United States.

name: St. John Paul II
title: Pope
short_bio: John Paul II led the Church through decades of global change and repeatedly called people to be unafraid in following Christ.

name: St. Teresa of Ávila
title: Doctor of the Church
short_bio: Teresa reformed Carmelite life and wrote classics on contemplative prayer. She taught practical perseverance in friendship with God.

name: St. Benedict
title: Abbot
short_bio: Benedict authored a rule balancing prayer, work, and community life. His monastic wisdom shaped Christian culture across centuries.

name: St. Clare of Assisi
title: Founder of the Poor Clares
short_bio: Inspired by Francis, Clare embraced a life of poverty and contemplative prayer. She guided her sisters with humility and strength.

name: St. Maximilian Kolbe
title: Priest and Martyr
short_bio: In Auschwitz, Maximilian Kolbe offered his life in place of another prisoner. He is remembered for sacrificial love and Marian devotion.

name: St. Cecilia
title: Virgin and Martyr
short_bio: St. Cecilia is traditionally honored as patron saint of musicians. Her witness reflects steadfast faith amid hardship.

name: Bl. Carlo Acutis
title: Blessed
short_bio: Carlo Acutis used technology to share devotion to the Eucharist and lived ordinary holiness as a teenager. He is a modern witness of joyful faith.
